note: Pinned, fixed requirement checklist for ALL runs of this task. Authored once from the bundled task spec so requirement_coverage uses a constant denominator and is comparable across runs. Do not regenerate per-run.

requirements.requirement: Reads CSV from stdin or a file path argument
requirements.how_to_verify: The tool accepts both a piped stdin stream and a file path.

requirements.requirement: filter transformation keeps rows matching a column condition
requirements.how_to_verify: A filter subcommand/flag (e.g. --filter "age>30") selects matching rows.

requirements.requirement: sort transformation orders by a column (ascending/descending)
requirements.how_to_verify: A sort subcommand/flag sorts by a named column in both directions.

requirements.requirement: aggregate transformation groups by a column and computes sum/mean/count
requirements.how_to_verify: An aggregate subcommand groups by one column and reduces another.

requirements.requirement: Outputs results as CSV to stdout or a specified file
requirements.how_to_verify: Output is CSV, written to stdout and to an output-file option.

requirements.requirement: Handles malformed CSV gracefully (skips bad rows, reports count)
requirements.how_to_verify: Bad rows are skipped and a count is reported rather than crashing.

requirements.requirement: Supports large files via streaming (not loading all into memory)
requirements.how_to_verify: Rows are processed incrementally, not read fully into memory.

requirements.requirement: Exit code 0 on success, 1 on error with message to stderr
requirements.how_to_verify: Success exits 0; errors exit 1 and write a message to stderr.
Running with --help prints usage and exits 0.

requirements.requirement: At least 3 tests covering filter, sort, and aggregate
requirements.how_to_verify: >= 3 tests exist and run (test_coverage > 0), covering the three transforms.
